brief_title: Short-Course Rifapentine-Isoniazid for Prevention of TB in Household Contacts
official_title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Open-Label Study Comparing 3-Month Weekly Rifapentine Plus Isoniazid (3HP) to 6-Month Daily Isoniazid (6H) for Tuberculosis Prevention in Household Contacts of Confirmed Pulmonary TB Cases
interventions: Rifapentine 900mg + Isoniazid 900mg weekly for 12 weeks (3HP regimen)
brief_summary: This Phase 3 study compares the efficacy and completion rates of a 3-month weekly rifapentine-isoniazid regimen to 6-month daily isoniazid for prevention of tuberculosis in household contacts of confirmed pulmonary TB cases. The primary endpoint is incidence of active TB over 24 months of follow-up.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Age >= 2 years (no upper age limit)

2. Household contact of a microbiologically confirmed pulmonary TB case within the past 90 days

3. Positive tuberculin skin test (>= 5 mm induration) or positive interferon-gamma release assay (IGRA)

4. No evidence of active tuberculosis disease (based on symptom screen, chest radiograph, and sputum if indicated)

5. No prior history of active tuberculosis disease

6. No current treatment for latent TB infection

7. Body weight >= 10 kg

8. ALT <= 2x upper limit of normal at screening

9. Not currently pregnant or planning pregnancy within 6 months

10. Written informed consent (or parental consent for minors)

11. Willing to attend all scheduled visits

12. HIV-positive individuals eligible if on stable ART
raw_exclusion_text:
1. Active tuberculosis disease (pulmonary or extrapulmonary)

2. Previous treatment for active or latent TB within the past 2 years

3. Known resistance to isoniazid or rifampicin in the index case

4. Severe hepatic disease or hepatitis (ALT > 5x ULN)

5. Current use of antiretroviral agents with significant interaction with rifapentine (e.g., protease inhibitors)

6. Known hypersensitivity to isoniazid, rifamycins, or rifapentine

7. Currently on treatment with drugs that have significant interactions with rifapentine

8. Porphyria

9. Pregnant or breastfeeding

inclusion_criteria.text: Household contact of a microbiologically confirmed pulmonary TB case within the past 90 days
Positive tuberculin skin test (>= 5 mm induration) or positive interferon-gamma release assay (IGRA)
No evidence of active tuberculosis disease (based on symptom screen, chest radiograph, and sputum if indicated)
No prior history of active tuberculosis disease
No current treatment for latent TB infection
ALT <= 2x upper limit of normal at screening
Not currently pregnant or planning pregnancy within 6 months
Written informed consent (or parental consent for minors)
Willing to attend all scheduled visits
HIV-positive individuals eligible if on stable ART

exclusion_criteria.text: Previous treatment for active or latent TB within the past 2 years
Known resistance to isoniazid or rifampicin in the index case
Severe hepatic disease or hepatitis (ALT > 5x ULN)
Current use of antiretroviral agents with significant interaction with rifapentine (e.g., protease inhibitors)
Known hypersensitivity to isoniazid, rifamycins, or rifapentine
Currently on treatment with drugs that have significant interactions with rifapentine

BRAC James P Grant School of Public Health TB Clinic
